author: @DrSteveMorreale
text: Somehow Google/Youtube stumbled me into your world and I LOVE IT! You are a joy to watch and learn from. I've been curious about Arduino and you described exactly the stumbling blocks to trying it and sliced right through any resistance. Now I just need to dream up a project. Thank you for your infectious enthusiasm for sharing knowledge!

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
* Edit, this is one of Andrea's Very lengthy comments, and she explains a bit about George. Mr Dingbatt. Congratulations on your Anniversary,and wishing you many more to come! * 

Woah!! You have been reading my Diary!!
This is fascinating and also  ~something I was going to ask you about!!
For myself, & especially my Hubby, who can change a light bulb, *Just!! Lmao 😅

Mind you, he is a brilliant musician although he won't admit to it,
 he's one of those people who makes playing Any instrument look completely Effortless!
 (* I am most Envious about this skill!)

Mr Dingbatt is a diamond, and we've been together mostly for 33years.

He is a most patient and kind bloke, and has become used to the various sick, injured, orphaned wildlife that I rescue, rehab and release!

A "Little" about the 
"Pigeon in residence".. 
George the Pigeon, who is utterly adorable, affectionate and sweet...

George the Pigeon, just a tiny bully and feathered menace, according to the man in my life..
 
He's been with me for a few years now, 
And despite having a
Pigeon 'special lady' of his own, he likes to spend as much time with me, as he's allowed!!

He doesn't like Men near me...
-Especially my poor Hubby, 
who he treats with absolute loathing, 
and a touch of contempt!

As I am not sure whether it's appropriate for  social media,
~ I think it's best if I don't mention details of the Unpleasantness that's been visited upon Mr Dingbatt, as its far worse than just pooping on him!

I shall have to leave it to your imagination,
and let you know that they are just as sentient and self aware as humans.... 

They can and Do Plan Ahead, they recognise different people, plus,, when they have a little spare time on their toes,~
~They are very capable of planning and executing complex, nasty and Hilarious revenge....

Most of my friends aren't too keen on George,
 - however they they don't know what a sweet, intelligent and cheeky, cheerful chap he is!!

Who, alongside Jasper, has kept me company, helped me fight depression, and given me the opportunity to gain insight into the Avian brain, as well as my own!! 
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX 😜🐕‍🦺🕊️

author: @jamesross1003
text: Great, one of those things I was curious about down.  Now for a Raspberry PI demo.  LOL, just kidding.  I am pretty sure I can handle the PI, but have been curious about SEEED.  Now I know.  Thanks very much.

author: @totherarf
text: If anyone is interested in doing it with solder and bits The Engineering Mindset on YT (nothing to do with me) has just covered PWM motor controllers (basic circuit and how to chose your component values) ..... might just be worth it to learn a bit?

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: I enjoyed your twist on using the seeed as the servo tester to run the esc, very clever, however a little detail was needed on the type of signal it was producing to control the esc. The signal is one of the most common and used as the pwm for servos signal. Normally stuff like this is too complex for video but this one is so simple to explain, and I feel you could do a bang up job, CHEERS! By the way just switching 2 of the 3 BLDC motor wires will reverse the motors direction, so a DPDT switch used to reverse a standard brushed motor will also work with a BLDC motor.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @bugz000
text: check out "Odrive" controllers, they know how many poles the motor has and use some voodoo magic to work out what position the motor is in so will stall at one coil for startup, they work so well they can actually hold position and act like they are spring loaded, cool stuff, but the main thing is they should fire up that motor no problem, no need to spin it to get it going :)

author: @richbuilds_com
text: That's a cracking bargain at £20! I'd be tempted to swap the arduino out for an esp32. So long as you pull the right libraries in the IDE they work identically to an arduino but have lots more power/features.

replies.author: @richbuilds_com
replies.text: looking into it, it's not a drop in replacement - didn't realise the centre board was a custom arduino board

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @richbuilds_com
text:
You have an closing comment (*/) on the first line of your code and never open it. I'm sure it's a copy / paste error, but I thought I'd mention it before you have to answer lots of 'your code doesn't work' comments :-)

edit: had a beer, got my open/closed the wrong way round :D

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one mate - cheers - I'll look at it later and correct it - it's a bit late here right now
